title: operculina alatipes
intro: Operculina alatipes, also known as the snake root or snake gourd, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The roots of Operculina alatipes have been found to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the roots are often dried and crushed to make a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules for treating a range of health conditions.
